branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Internal structural / framework-internal diagnostic; branch-blind algebraic or seed-level test

description: Continuum Limit Program (CLP) scores: four dimensionless families CLP-A/B/C/D in [0,1] derived from the per-N witness values on the nine-point dense-cell ladder N in {409, 1539, 2254, 3917, 6038, 9379, 14181, 20793, 28014}. The threshold for the family-level closure-domain claim is 0.70 on each of A, C, D; CLP-B is reported as the per-sub-component score with the heuristic threshold 0.50. CLP-D = 0.30*A + 0.40*B + 0.30*C is the weighted overall figure of merit. Per-component asymptotes are Symanzik-2 fits y(N) = g + c_2/N^2 with bootstrap 95%-CIs (n=2000 resamples).

per_N_witness_values.model_selection: AICc selects Symanzik-2 over free power-law and Symanzik-2+4 (Delta-AICc >= 4.6 on all four B/B4 sub-components)

data_source: Per-N witness values, family scores, and CLP-D assembly are derived from the nine-point dense-cell payload set (P0..P8 cross-regime ladder). The full per-component fit including Symanzik-2 + bootstrap diagnostics is bundled in outputs/clp_full_report.json (reproducer src/generate_clp_full_report.py); the gamma-convergence sub-component breakdown is in outputs/clp_c_gamma_convergence_detailed_audit.json (reproducer src/verify_clp_c_gamma_convergence_detailed.py); the full B/B4 component scan is in outputs/clp_full_families_extended_audit.json (reproducer src/verify_clp_full_families_extended.py).
data_source_caveat: CLP-A/C/D family scores are aggregate from the per-N Symanzik-2 fits with bootstrap-95% CIs over the nine-regime dense-cell ladder; CLP-B/B4 reports four sub-component asymptotes (absorption/locality/density/spectral) with per-component bootstrap-95% CIs rather than a single family-level closure score. The aggregate dual-threshold reading (0.70 for A/C/D; 0.50 for B sub-components) is the canonical pass criterion.
verdict: CLP-A CLOSED (0.81), CLP-C GAMMA_CONVERGED (0.85), CLP-D CLP_PROVEN (0.74). CLP-B sits at 0.60 with all four sub-components above the heuristic 0.50 threshold but the family-level 0.70 closure-domain reading does not apply; CLP-B is reported as a per-sub-component robustness diagnostic in the operator-convergence sense, with the Symanzik-2 + bootstrap reproducing the per-N witness values exactly.
